exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (V bar)
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a V-bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Setup: Attach a V-bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight. Stand facing the machine, feet about hip-width apart.
Grip and posture: Grasp the V-bar with a neutral (palms facing) grip. Step slightly back so the cable is taut, hinge very slightly at the hips, chest up, shoulders back, and eyes forward.
Starting position: Tuck your elbows close to your sides and bend the elbows so the V-bar is near chest/upper abdomen level. Keep your upper arms stationary and vertical throughout the movement.

Downward movement (concentric): Exhale and press the bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and the triceps are contracted. Keep the wrists neutral and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.
End position: Pause briefly at full extension without locking the elbows aggressively, maintaining tension on the triceps.

Return (eccentric): Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position by bending the elbows, controlling the weight to avoid momentum. Stop when the forearms reach roughly parallel to the floor or the bar returns to chest/upper abdomen level.

Technique tips: Keep the torso stable and avoid using body swing or stepping to generate force. If you feel the delts or upper back taking over, reduce weight and re-establish tight elbows at your sides.
Repetitions and safety: Perform 8–15 controlled reps per set depending on goal; choose a weight that allows strict form. Stop if you feel sharp pain in the elbow or wrist.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using a V-bar to extend the elbows and isolate the triceps; performed from a standing position with the upper arms held stationary at the sides. Emphasize a full elbow extension and controlled return to work the long and lateral heads of the triceps.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
Keep your torso tall, chest up, and elbows pinned to your sides; this is the starting position with arms nearly fully extended (no locked hyperextension).
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the forearms toward the shoulders while keeping the upper arms stationary.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top when the bar reaches shoulder level; avoid shrugging or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with a controlled motion until the arms are nearly extended.
Maintain strict form throughout—do not swing the torso or use excessive momentum; engage the core to stabilize and keep the delts relaxed.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with an underhand (supinated) grip to isolate the biceps by flexing the elbow and lifting the bar toward the shoulders, then lowering with control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Alternate Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending the elbow, then extend the arm back up to the starting position.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and hold a dumbbell in one hand, raising it above your head with the elbow bent.
Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending the elbow, then extend the arm back up to the starting position.
Alternate arms for each repetition, keeping the core engaged and the movements controlled.
exercises.description: The Dumbbell Seated Alternate Overhead Triceps Extension targets the triceps by extending the arm overhead while seated. It helps in strengthening and toning the upper arms.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
instructions: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, shoulders retracted and pressed into the bench for stability.
Grip the barbell with a close grip: hands about shoulder-width apart (index fingers roughly in line with the shoulders) or slightly narrower; keep wrists neutral and stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your sternum; keep a tight core and stable foot drive.
Inhale and lower the bar under control to the lower chest / sternum area, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body (about 45 degrees or less from the torso) to emphasize the triceps.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended.
Maintain wrist alignment and shoulder position throughout; avoid flaring the elbows wide and keep the movement vertical over the chest.
Rerack the bar with control after the set; use a spotter when lifting heavy or if you are near failure.
Progress by increasing load gradually, or modify grip width slightly to change emphasis; stop or reduce range if you feel joint pain (especially in wrists or elbows).
exercises.description: A compound supine pressing exercise performed with a narrow (close) grip to place primary emphasis on the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and anterior delts. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the chest and pressed back to full elbow extension.

exercises.name: Barbell JM-Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor, eyes roughly under the bar; use a spotter or safety pins if available.
instructions: Set up supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor, eyes roughly under the bar; use a spotter or safety pins if available.
Grip the bar with a narrow to shoulder-width grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower) and unrack the bar, arms extended over chest.
Keeping the elbows tucked but not pinned to the sides, slowly lower the bar in a slight arc toward the area between the forehead and upper chest, bending only at the elbows.
Stop the descent when the forearms are near the forehead/upper chest (comfortable range) and pause briefly without bouncing.
Drive the bar back up by extending the elbows, following the same slight arc until the arms are fully extended over the chest; avoid flaring the elbows outward.
Control the eccentric (lowering) and concentric (lifting) phases, breathe in on the descent and exhale as you press up.
Rack the bar under control at the top or have the spotter assist when finished; use light weight and strict form to protect the elbows and shoulders.
exercises.description: A lying barbell press that blends a close-grip bench press and skullcrusher motion to emphasize elbow extension; the bar is lowered in a slight arc toward the forehead/upper-chest area and pressed back up. Primarily trains the triceps while also recruiting the pectorals and front delts for stabilization and drive.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (V bar)
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a V-bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Setup: Attach a V-bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight. Stand facing the machine, feet about hip-width apart.
Grip and posture: Grasp the V-bar with a neutral (palms facing) grip. Step slightly back so the cable is taut, hinge very slightly at the hips, chest up, shoulders back, and eyes forward.
Starting position: Tuck your elbows close to your sides and bend the elbows so the V-bar is near chest/upper abdomen level. Keep your upper arms stationary and vertical throughout the movement.

Downward movement (concentric): Exhale and press the bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and the triceps are contracted. Keep the wrists neutral and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.
End position: Pause briefly at full extension without locking the elbows aggressively, maintaining tension on the triceps.

Return (eccentric): Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position by bending the elbows, controlling the weight to avoid momentum. Stop when the forearms reach roughly parallel to the floor or the bar returns to chest/upper abdomen level.

Technique tips: Keep the torso stable and avoid using body swing or stepping to generate force. If you feel the delts or upper back taking over, reduce weight and re-establish tight elbows at your sides.
Repetitions and safety: Perform 8–15 controlled reps per set depending on goal; choose a weight that allows strict form. Stop if you feel sharp pain in the elbow or wrist.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using a V-bar to extend the elbows and isolate the triceps; performed from a standing position with the upper arms held stationary at the sides. Emphasize a full elbow extension and controlled return to work the long and lateral heads of the triceps.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Close Grip Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced.
Grasp a barbell with a close underhand (supinated) grip — hands roughly 6–8 inches (inside shoulder width) — wrists neutral and palms facing up.
Let the arms hang fully extended so the bar rests at thigh level; pull shoulders slightly down and back to remove upper-back shrug.
Keeping the elbows pinned to your sides and stationary, exhale and curl the bar upward by contracting the biceps until the forearms approach the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top (about 0–1 second) while keeping the wrists straight and avoiding shoulder movement.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to the start position until the arms are fully extended.
Avoid swinging the torso or using hip drive; if you must lean back to lift, reduce the weight. Repeat for desired repetitions.
exercises.description: A standing barbell curl performed with a narrow, supinated grip to emphasize the biceps by shortening the elbow flexors while reducing shoulder involvement. Performed standing with controlled concentric and eccentric phases.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Triceps Pushdown
exercises.notes: Set a single D‑handle on the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single D‑handle on the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet about shoulder‑width apart; grasp the handle with one hand (palm facing down or neutral) and step back slightly so the cable is taut.
Tuck the working elbow to your side and keep the upper arm stationary (arm position: standing/neutral, not raised), with the forearm bent at about 90° to start.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and shoulders down, then extend the elbow to push the handle straight down until the arm is fully extended and the triceps are contracted; keep the wrist neutral.
Pause briefly at full extension, then slowly allow the cable to raise your hand back to the start position with controlled motion, stopping when the forearm reaches the starting angle.
Avoid swinging the torso, moving the upper arm away from your side, or using momentum; repeat for desired reps then switch arms.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pushdown performed from a high pulley that isolates the triceps by extending the elbow against constant cable resistance. The upper arm is kept stationary at the side while the forearm pushes the handle down to full extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse grip Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core engaged.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core engaged.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a pronated (palms facing down) grip and elbows straight but not locked; wrists should be neutral.
Keep your upper arms stationary at your sides and exhale as you flex your elbows to curl the weights upward toward your shoulders.
Pause and squeeze at the top of the movement, keeping the wrists stable and avoiding supination (turning the palms up).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled extension of the elbows.
Avoid swinging the torso or using momentum; maintain an upright posture and controlled tempo for each repetition.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell curl performed with a pronated (reverse) grip that emphasizes the forearm muscles (brachioradialis) while also working the biceps. The movement is a controlled elbow flexion with palms facing down throughout the motion.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Lying Triceps Extension Skull Crusher
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor for stability and your head near the end of the bench so the bar can be lowered toward your forehead.
Grip the barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip and press it up so your arms are fully extended above your chest; wrists should be neutral and stacked over elbows.
Keep your upper arms fixed and perpendicular to the floor (or slightly angled toward your head) throughout the set — only the forearm should move.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar by flexing the elbows, bringing the bar toward your forehead or just above your temples in a controlled arc.
Pause briefly when the bar is a few inches from your forehead (avoid actually hitting your head) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Exhale and extend the elbows to press the bar back up to the start position, locking out but avoiding hyperextension of the elbows.
Keep wrists straight and avoid flaring the elbows outward or letting the upper arms drift; use a spotter if loading heavy for safety.
Perform repetitions with slow, controlled tempo and stop the set if you feel shoulder or wrist pain — reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A supine barbell isolation exercise that works elbow extension to load the triceps by lowering the bar toward the forehead (skull crusher) and extending back up. Emphasizes controlled movement from the elbow while keeping the upper arms relatively stationary.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Bench Extension
exercises.notes: Sit upright near the middle of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright near the middle of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up one dumbbell and hold it vertically with both hands under the top plate (or hold the handle with palms supporting it), arms extended overhead.
Keep your upper arms close to your head and pointing forward (elbows in) throughout the movement to isolate the triceps.
Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending at the elbows until your forearms are roughly parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the triceps.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining a neutral spine and engaged core; do not let the elbows flare excessively outward.
Extend the elbows to press the dumbbell back up to the starting position overhead using control, avoiding locking the elbows aggressively at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while maintaining steady breathing (inhale lowering, exhale pressing) and controlled tempo.
If you feel shoulder pain, reduce range of motion or use a lighter weight; keep the torso stable and avoid leaning back to lift the weight.
exercises.description: Seated overhead dumbbell bench extension performed with a single dumbbell held by both hands to isolate the triceps. From a seated, upright position you lower the weight behind the head by flexing the elbows and then extend the elbows to return to the start.

exercises.name: Cable One Arm Triceps Pushdown
exercises.notes: Set a single D‑handle on the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single D‑handle on the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet about shoulder‑width apart; grasp the handle with one hand (palm facing down or neutral) and step back slightly so the cable is taut.
Tuck the working elbow to your side and keep the upper arm stationary (arm position: standing/neutral, not raised), with the forearm bent at about 90° to start.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and shoulders down, then extend the elbow to push the handle straight down until the arm is fully extended and the triceps are contracted; keep the wrist neutral.
Pause briefly at full extension, then slowly allow the cable to raise your hand back to the start position with controlled motion, stopping when the forearm reaches the starting angle.
Avoid swinging the torso, moving the upper arm away from your side, or using momentum; repeat for desired reps then switch arms.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable pushdown performed from a high pulley that isolates the triceps by extending the elbow against constant cable resistance. The upper arm is kept stationary at the side while the forearm pushes the handle down to full extension.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (V bar)
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulley to the high position and attach a V‑bar.
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the high position and attach a V‑bar. Select an appropriate weight that allows controlled reps.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip‑width apart and a slight stagger if preferred for balance; grasp the V‑bar with a neutral (palms facing each other) grip.
Start with your elbows bent ~90°, upper arms close to your sides, and a slight forward lean at the hips so your torso is stable and shoulders are over your hips.
Brace your core and keep your upper arms stationary; exhale and press the V‑bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight but not locked aggressively.
Squeeze the triceps at the bottom of the movement for a brief pause (1 second) while maintaining neutral wrists.
Inhale and slowly return the V‑bar to the starting position under control, allowing the elbows to bend until the forearms are roughly parallel to the floor or reach the chosen start depth.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding use of momentum.
Common form cues: keep elbows tucked to your sides (do not let them drift forward or flare out), maintain neutral wrists, and avoid leaning back or swinging the body to complete reps.
exercises.description: Standing cable triceps pushdown using a V‑bar targets the triceps through elbow extension while the V‑grip emphasizes the medial head and allows a neutral wrist position. Performed from a high pulley, the movement is a strict elbow hinge with minimal shoulder movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Alternate Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (standing position). Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing forward (supinated grip).
Keep your elbows fixed close to your torso and shoulders down; do not let elbows drift forward. Maintain a neutral wrist (no excessive bending).
Exhale and curl the right dumbbell upward by flexing the elbow until the dumbbell reaches shoulder level; squeeze the biceps at the top. Keep the upper arm stationary — only the forearm should move.
Inhale and slowly lower the right dumbbell back to the starting position with a controlled eccentric motion.
Repeat the same curl pattern on the left arm while the right arm rests at your side, then alternate sides in a smooth, controlled rhythm (alternate repetitions).
Avoid using momentum or swinging the torso; if you find yourself swinging, reduce the weight. Continue to breathe steadily: exhale on each curl, inhale on each lower.
exercises.description: Standing alternate dumbbell biceps curls performed one arm at a time to isolate elbow flexion and build the biceps. Performed with controlled concentric curls and slow eccentric lowering while keeping the torso stable.

exercises.name: Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your chest and front of your upper arms pressed firmly against the angled pad and feet flat on the floor (seated position).
Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
Brace your core, keep your torso still and shoulders relaxed (no shrugging), and ensure your wrists remain neutral throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the movement with the biceps; keep the upper arms locked to the pad so only the forearms move.
Pause briefly at the top when the bar is near shoulder level and you feel maximal biceps contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the start position with controlled tension until your arms are near full extension but not locked out.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form (no swinging or lifting the torso) and using a weight that allows full control.
If wrists or elbows feel strained, reduce weight or use an EZ bar to improve wrist comfort; stop if you feel sharp pain.
exercises.description: Seated barbell preacher curl performed on an angled preacher bench to isolate the biceps by preventing shoulder movement; the upper arms remain supported on the pad while you curl the barbell. Focus is on controlled concentric and eccentric motion to maximize biceps activation.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (SZ bar)
exercises.notes: Attach an SZ bar to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach an SZ bar to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight forward lean from the hips; brace your core.

Grasp the SZ bar with a neutral/overhand grip (hands about shoulder-width), thumbs wrapped around the bar, and bring your elbows tight to your sides. Your upper arms should be stationary and vertical.
Start with your forearms bent (bar near chest/upper abdomen) and inhale.

Exhale and press the bar downward by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and the triceps are contracted; keep wrists neutral and avoid flaring your elbows or using torso momentum.
Pause briefly at full extension to maximize triceps contraction.
Slowly control the bar back up to the starting position by flexing the elbows until forearms return to vertical; maintain elbow position throughout.
Repeat for desired repetitions while maintaining steady posture and controlled movement.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using an SZ (cambered) bar to extend the elbows and isolate the triceps; performed from a high pulley with controlled tempo to emphasize the triceps contraction.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing forward (supinated grip) and arms fully extended.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and fixed in position throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by bending your elbows until the weights reach shoulder level, squeezing the biceps at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining upright posture and avoid leaning back or using momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with full elbow extension under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on strict form and minimizing shoulder or torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the biceps by flexing the elbow against resistance while standing upright; perform controlled reps without swinging to maximize tension on the biceps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full squat (with rack)
exercises.notes: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
instructions: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
Stand under the bar with it resting across your upper back/traps (not on the neck). Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width.
Unrack the bar by straightening your legs to lift it off the hooks, then take 1–2 small steps back so the rack is clear; stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core (tighten abs), keep chest up and eyes forward. Inhale and begin the descent by pushing hips back and bending knees, keeping knees tracking over toes.
Lower to full depth (hips at or below parallel to the knees) while maintaining a neutral spine and weight distributed through the mid-foot and heels.
Drive through the heels and mid-foot, exhale, and extend hips and knees to return to a fully upright standing position while maintaining a tight core and upright torso.
Step forward and carefully re-rack the bar on the hooks. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat performed from a rack where the lifter unracks the bar, steps back and squats to full depth (hips at or below parallel) before returning to standing. This compound lower-body movement primarily loads the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability.

exercises.name: Sled 45 degrees One Leg Press
exercises.notes: Place one foot flat, centered on the footplate about hip-width apart; keep the other foot off the plate or braced lightly on the sled frame so it does not assist.
instructions: Sit on the 45° leg press sled and lie back with your head and lower back firmly against the pad (supine on the incline).
Place one foot flat, centered on the footplate about hip-width apart; keep the other foot off the plate or braced lightly on the sled frame so it does not assist.
Grip the side handles to stabilize your torso and ensure your hips remain pressed into the seat throughout the movement.
Unlock the sled safety (if applicable) while maintaining control of the plate with your working leg.
Inhale, then exhale as you press through the heel of the foot on the plate, extending the knee and driving the sled away until the leg is nearly straight — stop short of locking the knee.
Slowly lower the sled by bending the knee and hip under control until your working knee reaches about a 90° angle (or a comfortable depth), keeping your back pressed into the pad.
Repeat for the desired reps, then return the sled to the safety catches before switching legs.
Coaching tips: keep a neutral spine against the pad, avoid letting the non-working leg push or assist, and use a controlled tempo to protect the knee joint.
exercises.description: Single-leg press performed on a 45° sled to load one leg at a time, emphasizing knee extension while providing hip drive. Performed supine on the inclined sled, it increases unilateral quad strength and balance.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the platform and heels hanging off the back; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and stand tall (upright torso).
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or aerobic platform with the balls of both feet on the platform and heels hanging off the back; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and stand tall (upright torso).
Position feet about hip-width apart and keep a slight, natural bend in the knees (do not lock the knees).
Engage your abs to stabilize the torso and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise your heels by pressing through the balls of your feet until you are fully on your toes and calves are fully contracted; avoid rocking the torso forward.
Pause for 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the step level until you feel a stretch in the calves (control the descent — do not drop abruptly).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping movements smooth and maintaining balance; if needed, reduce weight or use a rail/wall for light support.
exercises.description: Standing calf raise performed on a raised step while holding dumbbells at the sides to load the lower leg; the movement raises the heels by plantarflexing the ankles to target the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves). Emphasis is on controlled concentric raise and slow eccentric lowering below the step for stretch.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a front-rack position on a squat rack at about collarbone height; approach the bar and step under it so it rests on the anterior deltoids and across the clavicles in a cross-arm or clean grip.
instructions: Set the barbell in a front-rack position on a squat rack at about collarbone height; approach the bar and step under it so it rests on the anterior deltoids and across the clavicles in a cross-arm or clean grip.
Establish your grip (cross-arm shown in video) and lift the chest: elbows high and pointed forward to create a stable shelf for the bar; keep the upper back tight and shoulders engaged.
Unrack the bar by standing up and taking 1-2 steps back; position feet shoulder-width apart with toes turned slightly outward and weight distributed on the midfoot/heel.
Brace your core and inhale deeply to create intra-abdominal pressure; keep the chest up and eyes forward throughout the movement.
Initiate the squat by bending at the hips and knees simultaneously, sitting back slightly but maintaining an upright torso so the bar stays balanced over the mid-foot.

Descend until the thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or to a comfortable depth while maintaining an upright torso and neutral spine). Ensure knees track in line with the toes and do not cave inward.
Drive the movement upward by pressing through the heels and midfoot, extending the knees and hips while keeping the chest lifted and elbows high; exhale as you stand to full extension.
Reset your breath and posture between reps if needed; maintain controlled tempo and avoid letting the torso collapse forward or the heels rise.
After the set, carefully step forward and re‑rack the bar on the squat rack ensuring control and proper placement before fully releasing.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat variation that emphasizes the quadriceps by keeping the torso more upright and shifting load to the front of the legs; the bar is held in a front-rack or cross-arm position. It trains lower-body strength while also demanding core and upper-back stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Split Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell on a squat rack at upper‑chest height and load appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the barbell on a squat rack at upper‑chest height and load appropriate weight. Step under the bar and rest it across your upper back/traps with a secure, pronated grip; unrack the bar and take a small step back.

Position your feet in a split stance: one foot forward (working leg) and the other foot back with toes on the floor and heel elevated slightly; keep a distance so the front knee can bend to about 90 degrees at the bottom.
Brace your core and keep your torso upright and chest lifted throughout the movement; gaze forward to help maintain posture.

Slowly lower your body by bending the front knee and pushing the hips back slightly until the front thigh is around parallel to the floor or you reach a comfortable depth; the rear knee should lower toward the floor but not necessarily touch.
Ensure the front knee tracks in line with your toes (avoid collapsing inward) and keep weight primarily through the front heel and midfoot.
Drive through the front heel to extend the front knee and hip, returning to the starting split stance. Exhale on the drive.
Complete the desired number of reps on the working leg, then carefully step the back foot forward, re‑rack the bar, reset, and repeat with the opposite leg.
exercises.description: A unilateral, loaded lower‑body exercise performed in a staggered (split) stance with a barbell on the upper back that emphasizes the front leg's quadriceps while also working the glutes and hamstrings for hip control and the core for stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at shoulder height and load desired weight.
instructions: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at shoulder height and load desired weight. Step under the bar and place it across your upper back/traps, grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Unrack the bar and step back from the rack, then position an elevated platform or weight plate under the balls of your feet so your heels can hang off the edge; stand with feet hip-width apart.
Brace your core (abs) and maintain an upright torso; keep knees slightly soft (not fully locked) throughout the movement.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, coming up onto your toes and fully contracting the calves.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the calves, keeping the barbell stable on your traps and upper back (traps engaged to support the load).
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the level of the platform until you feel a stretch in the calves.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then carefully step forward and rack the barbell with control or use a spotter/safety pins if needed.
exercises.description: Standing calf raise performed with a barbell across the upper back; raise heels by plantarflexing at the ankle to work the calf muscles, then lower heels below the platform for a stretch. The barbell and core help stabilize the body during each repetition.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Single Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the lever pad sits just above the back of your ankle and the pivot aligns with your knee joint.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the lever pad sits just above the back of your ankle and the pivot aligns with your knee joint.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with hips and pelvis firmly against the pad, one foot hooked under the padded lever and the other leg extended and relaxed.
Grasp the handles or edge of the bench for stability and brace your core to prevent your hips from lifting.
Start with the working leg nearly straight (slight natural bend in the knee) and exhale as you flex the knee to curl the heel toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause briefly at the top when you feel a full hamstring contraction without excessive hip extension.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start position, fully controlling the eccentric phase without letting the weight slam down.
Keep the non-working leg relaxed and hips pressed into the bench; avoid lifting or thrusting the hips to cheat the movement.
Complete the desired reps, then switch legs and repeat for the other side.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) single-leg curl performed on a lever/lying leg curl machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee to bring the heel toward the glutes. Movement emphasizes a controlled concentric curl and a slow eccentric return while keeping the hips pinned to the bench.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Secure one end of a resistance band to a low, sturdy anchor (door anchor at floor level or heavy fixed object) behind you and loop the free end around the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Secure one end of a resistance band to a low, sturdy anchor (door anchor at floor level or heavy fixed object) behind you and loop the free end around the ankle of the working leg.
Stand upright facing away from the anchor with feet hip‑width apart and the working leg slightly forward to create initial tension in the band; keep a soft bend in the standing knee for balance.
Brace your abs and keep your torso tall and hips level — avoid excessive forward lean or hip drive throughout the movement.
Keeping the thigh stationary, exhale and bend the working knee to curl the heel up toward your glutes, squeezing the hamstrings at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly (1–2 seconds) at full knee flexion while maintaining control and avoiding hip extension.
Inhale and slowly extend the knee to return the foot to the starting position under control, maintaining band tension.
Perform the desired number of reps, then switch sides and repeat with the other leg.
exercises.description: A standing single‑leg resistance band curl that isolates the hamstrings by pulling the heel toward the glutes against band tension. Performed upright with the band anchored low to provide constant resistance through knee flexion.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine and fasten the cuff around the ankle of the working leg; position your feet hip-width apart.
Hold the machine for balance (hands on a handle or the frame) and take a slight forward lean from the hips with a neutral spine; brace your abs.
Keep the supporting knee slightly bent and the working leg straight or with a small bend at the knee throughout the movement.
Drive the working heel backward and slightly upward by contracting the glute until the leg is roughly in line with the torso or slightly behind — avoid arching the lower back or rotating the hips.
Pause and squeeze the glute 1–2 seconds at the top of the movement.
Slowly return the leg to the start position under control, maintaining tension in the glute and hamstrings.
Complete the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat.
exercises.description: Standing cable hip extension performed with an ankle cuff on a low pulley; extend the working leg backward by contracting the glute to drive hip extension while the torso remains stable. The movement emphasizes the gluteus maximus with supporting action from the hamstrings.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full Squat (Side POV)
exercises.notes: Set the barbell on a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the barbell on a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Step under the bar and rest it across your upper back on the traps, hands gripping the bar just outside shoulder width.
Unrack the bar by straightening your legs, take 1–2 steps back, and position your feet shoulder-width apart with toes turned slightly outward.
Take a deep breath and brace your abs to create a rigid torso with a neutral spine and chest lifted throughout the movement.
Initiate the descent by pushing your hips back and bending your knees simultaneously, keeping your knees tracking in line with your toes.
Lower in a controlled manner until you reach a full depth (hips below parallel / hip crease below the knee) while maintaining an upright torso and neutral spine.
Drive through your heels and mid-foot, exhale as you extend your knees and hips to stand back up, squeezing the glutes at the top without hyperextending the lower back.
Reset your breath and core brace at the top, then repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Maintain tightness in the traps to support the bar and prevent excessive bar movement.
exercises.description: Barbell full squat is a loaded, compound lower-body movement performed standing with a barbell across the upper back; it emphasizes deep knee and hip flexion to work the front thighs. The exercise also requires core and posterior chain stabilization to return to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height.
instructions: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height. Step under the bar and position it across the front of your shoulders (front of delts/upper chest) using either a clean (front-rack) grip or a cross-arm hold.
Stand up to unrack the bar, take one or two small steps back, and place your feet about shoulder-width apart with toes pointed slightly out.
Raise your elbows so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and brace your abs to keep the torso upright throughout the movement.
Inhale, push your hips back slightly and bend at the knees to descend into a squat; keep your chest up, elbows high, and knees tracking over your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs reach at least parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows) while maintaining a neutral spine.
Exhale and drive through your heels and midfoot to extend your knees and hips, returning to the standing start position with control.
Maintain tight abs and high elbows on every rep; avoid letting the knees cave inward or the torso fold forward. Rack the bar safely when finished.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat where the barbell is held across the front of the shoulders, emphasizing upright torso and knee extension to target the quadriceps. Performed by descending into a squat and driving back up while keeping elbows high and core braced.

exercises.name: Cable Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Set the cable pulley to the lowest position and attach an ankle cuff to the working ankle; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the lowest position and attach an ankle cuff to the working ankle; select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine and hold the frame or a handle with both hands for balance; feet hip-width apart, slight forward lean from the hips.
Brace your core, keep your chest up and hips square to the machine throughout the movement.
Begin with a small bend in the standing leg and the working leg slightly behind you with tension on the cable.
Drive the working leg straight back by extending at the hip—focus on squeezing the glute at the top of the movement; keep the knee mostly straight or with a slight bend.
Do not allow the torso to rotate or the lower back to hyperextend; movement should come from the hip joint.
Pause briefly at full extension while maintaining tension, then control the leg back to the starting position without letting the weight slam.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg and repeat.
exercises.description: Standing unilateral hip extension performed with a low cable and ankle cuff; extend the working leg backward against resistance to target the gluteus maximus while the hamstrings and lower back assist. The exercise is done standing with torso stabilized and the movement driven from the hip.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Single Leg Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the leg press seat with your back firmly against the backrest and both hands holding the side handles for stability.
instructions: Sit upright on the leg press seat with your back firmly against the backrest and both hands holding the side handles for stability.
Place the ball of one foot flat and centered on the lower portion of the foot plate so your heel can move freely; keep the other foot off the plate or resting lightly.
Start with the working ankle dorsiflexed (toes pulled toward shin) so the heel is lowered toward the platform.
Press through the ball of the foot by pointing the toes and extending the ankle (plantarflex) until the calf is fully contracted; avoid driving through the knee or lifting the hips.
Pause briefly at full contraction, then slowly lower the heel under control back to the start (dorsiflex) to feel a stretch in the calf.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat. Maintain a controlled tempo and keep the back pressed into the seat throughout.
exercises.description: A single‑leg seated calf press performed on a lever leg‑press machine focusing on ankle plantarflexion to isolate the calf muscles. The exerciser pushes the platform away using the ball of one foot while keeping the torso supported by the seat.

exercises.name: Lever Angled Leg Press
exercises.notes: Step 1: Load an appropriate weight and set the sled's safety/stop pins; adjust the backrest so your back and head sit fully supported when lying supine.
instructions: Step 1: Load an appropriate weight and set the sled's safety/stop pins; adjust the backrest so your back and head sit fully supported when lying supine.

Step 2: Sit and lie back on the angled sled with your back and head firmly against the pad, feet flat and shoulder-width on the platform (place feet a bit higher on the platform to emphasize glutes/hamstrings, lower to emphasize quads).
Step 3: Grip the side handles, disengage the safety catch, and start with knees bent to about 90 degrees (or slightly less) without letting your lower back lift or round.
Step 4: Exhale and press through your heels and mid-foot to extend the knees, pushing the platform away until legs are near straight but do NOT lock the knees.

Step 5: Pause briefly at the top with tension maintained, then inhale and slowly lower the sled in a controlled manner back to the start position (stop when knees reach ~90 degrees or slightly past, avoiding deep flexion that causes the lower back to round).
Step 6: Repeat for the desired reps; after the set, re-engage the safety catch before removing your feet from the platform.
exercises.description: A supine angled lever leg press performed on a sled machine that primarily targets the quads by extending the knees against the platform; foot placement and platform height can shift emphasis toward the glutes and hamstrings. The back and head remain supported while you press the sled away with controlled motion.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the hip support pad allows your hips to stay flat on the bench and the lever pivot aligns with your knees; select appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the hip support pad allows your hips to stay flat on the bench and the lever pivot aligns with your knees; select appropriate weight.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your knees just off the edge, chest supported, and hook your lower calves/ankles under the padded lever.
Grip the handles or edge of the bench for stability and keep your hips pressed into the bench throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl your heels up toward your glutes by flexing the knees until you reach a full but comfortable contraction.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the hamstrings and glutes.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start with controlled extension of the knees, avoiding letting the hips rise.
Perform repetitions with steady tempo, focusing on full range of motion and control rather than momentum.
exercises.description: Prone (face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by bending the knees to lift a pad toward the glutes. The movement emphasizes knee flexion with a controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Secure one end of a resistance band to a low, sturdy anchor (door anchor at floor level or heavy fixed object) behind you and loop the free end around the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Secure one end of a resistance band to a low, sturdy anchor (door anchor at floor level or heavy fixed object) behind you and loop the free end around the ankle of the working leg.
Stand upright facing away from the anchor with feet hip‑width apart and the working leg slightly forward to create initial tension in the band; keep a soft bend in the standing knee for balance.
Brace your abs and keep your torso tall and hips level — avoid excessive forward lean or hip drive throughout the movement.
Keeping the thigh stationary, exhale and bend the working knee to curl the heel up toward your glutes, squeezing the hamstrings at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly (1–2 seconds) at full knee flexion while maintaining control and avoiding hip extension.
Inhale and slowly extend the knee to return the foot to the starting position under control, maintaining band tension.
Perform the desired number of reps, then switch sides and repeat with the other leg.
exercises.description: A standing single‑leg resistance band curl that isolates the hamstrings by pulling the heel toward the glutes against band tension. Performed upright with the band anchored low to provide constant resistance through knee flexion.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
